body.title: Reading — book ingestion + two-pass deposit-then-encode into the graph
body.summary: Reading turns books into graph memory. A book is opened and read chunk-by-chunk; each chunk is pushed to Transient Working Memory and extracted into nodes. Crucially, raw extraction is DEPOSITION, not encoding (D170): deposited READ_* nodes only become reasoning-useful after a second pass — embed, link (cosine + BFS edges), spine (sequential narrative thread), interp (interpretive edges), arousal (VAD encoding). Stew salience is set above the Narrative Engine's force-run threshold so book content is processed on the next NE cycle without a manual trigger. In UnseenUniversity the chunk/extract pipeline is extracted into the portable reader DEVICE; Igor-side book/ebook handling and the integration pass remain in the igor tools.

key_concepts.name: deposit != encode (D170)
key_concepts.what: raw extraction deposits READ_* nodes; a second 5-step pass (embed/link/spine/interp/arousal) makes them usable for reasoning

key_concepts.name: stew salience above NE threshold
key_concepts.what: book chunks pushed to TWM at a salience above the Narrative Engine force-run floor so they are synthesized automatically

key_concepts.name: reader device (portable)
key_concepts.what: chunking, node extraction, URI resolution and blob caching are extracted into the reader rack device

key_concepts.name: ebook ingestion (Igor-side)
key_concepts.what: format handling (epub/mobi/azw/pdf), tokenization, position state, and the reading-integration pass live in igor tools

body.status_notes: Reader DEVICE is the lead/portable path; igor/tools/* is the Igor-side reading path that still owns ebook formats and the integration pass. Calibre IDs, experiment-roadmap numbers and the drain-runner case (D179/D181) in the source doc are point-in-time and were NOT carried forward. cortex.py _deposit_nodes() is the canonical write primitive for book nodes.
